content.problemStatement:
Let's call a sequence good if the sum of all its elements is 000.

You have a sequence of integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. You may perform any number of operations on this sequence (including zero). In one operation, you should choose a valid index iii and decrease AiA_iAi​ by iii. Can you make the sequence good using these operations?
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with an integer N, the length of the sequence. The next line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if it is possible to make the sum of the sequence equal to 0 using the allowed operations, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: sum = -1 < 0, impossible → NO. Test case 2: sum = 3 >= 0, always possible (e.g., subtract 1 three times from A1) → YES.

solution.approach: Let S be the sum of the original array. After performing operations we obtain Ai' = Ai - ki * i where ki >= 0 is an integer. The final sum is S - Σ(ki * i). To make the final sum zero we need Σ(ki * i) = S. The left side is a non‑negative linear combination of the numbers 1,2,…,N. Because the set contains 1, any non‑negative integer S can be expressed (e.g., take k1 = S, all other ki = 0). Therefore the condition is simply S >= 0. If the original sum is negative, it is impossible; otherwise it is always possible.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case to compute the sum, overall O(ΣN) which is at most O(T·N).
